units.content: Fadini et al. conduct the most recent AF-vs-ESMFold comparison on 1,666 monomers and 994 dimers, in NAR Genomics and Bioinformatics 8(1):lqag002, 2026
This study is the most recent and largest AF-vs-ESMFold comparison in the observational literature
The same mitigation as Savojardo (v6.2.15) applies to this reference
Fadini et al. is an observational, post-hoc study, not a pre-registered architectural predictor
Fadini et al. is distinguished from claim 8a because claim 8a is derived from attribute-set arithmetic, not empirical post-hoc evaluation
The observational and post-hoc nature of Fadini et al. mitigates the FTO risk despite its recency and scale
